nodes.title: Lesson 12-1
nodes.description: Recognize patterns in numerical, geometric, and real-world sequences and express them as conjectures; Determine whether a given conjecture is true or false

nodes.title: Lesson 12-10
nodes.description: Find the distance from a point to a line in the coordinate plane

nodes.title: Lesson 12-2
nodes.description: Determine the truth value of compound statements formed by conjunction and disjunction; Identify the hypothesis and conclusion of a conditional statement and write the converse, inverse, and contrapositive

nodes.title: Lesson 12-3
nodes.description: Distinguish between inductive and deductive reasoning in given arguments; Apply the Law of Detachment and the Law of Syllogism to draw valid conclusions from conditional statements

nodes.title: Lesson 12-4
nodes.description: Apply geometric postulates about points, lines, and planes to justify statements; Write two-column proofs using given information, definitions, and properties

nodes.title: Lesson 12-5
nodes.description: Complete two-column proofs by supplying missing statements and reasons

nodes.title: Lesson 12-6
nodes.description: Write two-column proofs to justify angle relationships including congruent supplements, congruent complements, and right angles

nodes.title: Lesson 12-7
nodes.description: Classify angle pairs formed by two lines cut by a transversal as alternate interior, alternate exterior, corresponding, or consecutive interior angles; Use properties of parallel lines and transversals to find unknown angle measures

nodes.title: Lesson 12-8
nodes.description: Calculate the slope of a line through two given points and interpret its meaning; Write equations of lines in slope-intercept form that are parallel or perpendicular to a given line and pass through a given point

nodes.title: Lesson 12-9
nodes.description: Use angle relationships formed by a transversal to prove that two lines are parallel; Apply parallel line theorems to solve real-world and geometric problems

nodes.title: Lesson 13-1
nodes.description: Graph the image of a figure after a reflection over a given line on the coordinate plane; Determine the coordinates of the image of a point after a reflection in a horizontal or vertical line

nodes.title: Lesson 13-2
nodes.description: Determine whether a given transformation between two figures is a translation and justify the answer; Find the translation vector that maps a preimage to its image and apply it

nodes.title: Lesson 13-3
nodes.description: Graph the image of a polygon after a rotation of 90°, 180°, or 270° about a given point on the coordinate plane; Analyze properties preserved under rotations and identify common errors in reasoning about rotations

nodes.title: Lesson 13-4
nodes.description: Graph the image of a figure after a glide reflection or other composition of transformations on the coordinate plane; Identify a single transformation that is equivalent to a composition of two reflections

nodes.title: Lesson 13-5
nodes.description: Determine whether a regular polygon will tessellate the plane by examining its interior angle measure; Determine whether a tessellation can be created from a given figure and describe the transformation(s) used

nodes.title: Lesson 13-6
nodes.description: Identify and draw lines of symmetry for two-dimensional figures; Determine whether a figure has rotational symmetry and justify the answer

nodes.title: Lesson 14-1
nodes.description: Apply the Triangle Angle-Sum Theorem to find unknown interior angle measures; Use the Exterior Angle Theorem to relate exterior and remote interior angles

nodes.title: Lesson 14-2
nodes.description: Identify all congruent corresponding parts of polygons and write correct congruence statements; Write two-column proofs and paragraph proofs to show that two triangles are congruent

nodes.title: Lesson 14-3
nodes.description: Write two-column, paragraph, and flow proofs to prove triangle congruence using the SSS and SAS postulates; Identify the included angle and apply the SAS Congruence Postulate correctly

nodes.title: Lesson 14-4
nodes.description: Write two-column, paragraph, and flow proofs to prove triangle congruence using the ASA and AAS theorems; Use properties of parallel lines, midpoints, and angle bisectors to find congruent parts needed for ASA or AAS proofs

nodes.title: Lesson 14-5
nodes.description: Apply the Leg-Leg (LL), Hypotenuse-Angle (HA), Leg-Angle (LA), and Hypotenuse-Leg (HL) theorems to prove right triangles congruent; Write two-column proofs and paragraph proofs for right triangle congruence in real-world contexts

nodes.title: Lesson 14-6
nodes.description: Write two-column proofs using the Isosceles Triangle Theorem and its converse; Solve for unknown variables using properties of isosceles and equilateral triangles

nodes.title: Lesson 14-7
nodes.description: Position and label triangles on the coordinate plane using given side lengths and properties; Write coordinate proofs for geometric statements about triangles

nodes.title: Lesson 2-1
nodes.description: Translate verbal sentences and real-world situations into algebraic equations and formulas;

nodes.title: Lesson 2-2
nodes.description: Solve one-step equations involving addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division using the Properties of Equality;

nodes.title: Lesson 2-3
nodes.description: Solve multi-step linear equations using properties of equality and check solutions by substitution;

nodes.title: Lesson 2-4
nodes.description: Solve linear equations that have the variable on both sides of the equation, including those with fractions and grouping symbols;

nodes.title: Lesson 2-5
nodes.description: Solve absolute value equations of the form; ; Graph the solution set of an absolute value equation on a number line and write absolute value equations from graphs

nodes.title: Lesson 2-6
nodes.description: Solve proportions algebraically, including those with variable expressions in numerators and denominators;

nodes.title: Lesson 2-7
nodes.description: Solve literal equations and formulas for a specified variable using inverse operations;

nodes.title: Lesson 3-1
nodes.description: Express a relation as a table, a graph, a mapping, and a set of ordered pairs and identify the domain and range;

nodes.title: Lesson 3-2
nodes.description: Determine whether a relation represented as a graph, table, mapping, set of ordered pairs, or equation is a function;

nodes.title: Lesson 3-3
nodes.description: Determine whether a function represented by a graph, equation, table, or real-world situation is discrete, continuous, or neither;

nodes.title: Lesson 3-4
nodes.description: Estimate and interpret the x- and y-intercepts of a function from its graph and describe where the function is positive and negative;

nodes.title: Lesson 3-5
nodes.description: Identify line symmetry in the graph of a function and determine the intervals where a function is increasing, decreasing, or constant;

nodes.title: Lesson 3-6
nodes.description: Sketch graphs of real-world functions from verbal descriptions, identifying intercepts, intervals of increase and decrease, end behavior, extrema, and symmetry;

nodes.title: Lesson 4-1
nodes.description: Graph linear equations by making a table of values and by finding and plotting the x- and y-intercepts;

nodes.title: Lesson 4-2
nodes.description: Calculate the rate of change from tables, graphs, and real-world data and compute the slope of a line passing through two given points; ; Interpret slope and rate of change in real-world contexts including grade, elevation, and positioning

nodes.title: Lesson 4-3
nodes.description: Write linear equations in slope-intercept form given the slope and y-intercept and rewrite linear equations from standard form into slope-intercept form;

nodes.title: Lesson 4-4
nodes.description: Describe translations, dilations, and reflections of linear functions in relation to the parent function f(x) = x;

nodes.title: Lesson 4-5
nodes.description: Determine whether a given sequence is arithmetic and justify the reasoning using the common difference;

nodes.title: Lesson 4-6
nodes.description: Graph piecewise-defined functions and step functions (greatest integer functions) and identify their domain and range;

nodes.title: Lesson 4-7
nodes.description: Describe translations, dilations, and reflections of absolute value functions from their equations and graph absolute value functions;

nodes.title: Lesson 5-1
nodes.description: Write linear equations in slope-intercept form given a point and a slope and given two points;

nodes.title: Lesson 5-2
nodes.description: Write linear equations in point-slope form given a point and a slope or two points and convert equations between point-slope, slope-intercept, and standard forms;

nodes.title: Lesson 5-3
nodes.description: Analyze scatter plots to determine whether they show positive, negative, or no correlation and interpret the meaning in context;

nodes.title: Lesson 5-4
nodes.description: Distinguish between correlation and causation in real-world contexts and explain reasoning for whether a relationship is merely correlated or causal, identifying possible lurking variables;

nodes.title: Lesson 5-5
nodes.description: Use technology to calculate the equation of a best-fit line and the correlation coefficient for a bivariate data set and interpret the correlation coefficient r in context;

nodes.title: Lesson 5-6
nodes.description: Find the inverse of a relation given its graph, table, or set of ordered pairs and find the inverse of a linear function algebraically using the switch-and-solve method;

nodes.title: Lesson 6-1
nodes.description: Graph the solution set of a one-step inequality on a number line and write an inequality that matches a given number-line graph;

nodes.title: Lesson 6-2
nodes.description: Solve multi-step linear inequalities using the Distributive Property, combining like terms, and inverse operations;

nodes.title: Lesson 6-3
nodes.description: Solve compound inequalities involving "and" and "or" and express the solution algebraically;

nodes.title: Lesson 6-4
nodes.description: Solve absolute value inequalities and rewrite them as compound inequalities using and or or;

nodes.title: Lesson 6-5
nodes.description: Graph linear inequalities in two variables written in slope-intercept and standard form and write linear inequalities to model real-world constraints; ; Write systems of inequalities that correspond to a given graph and determine whether an ordered pair is a solution to a system without graphing

nodes.title: Lesson 7-1
nodes.description: Graph systems of linear equations and determine the number of solutions; classify systems as consistent or inconsistent and as independent or dependent;

nodes.title: Lesson 7-2
nodes.description: Solve systems of linear equations using the substitution method;

nodes.title: Lesson 7-3
nodes.description: Solve systems of linear equations using elimination by addition or subtraction and recognize when adding or subtracting equations will eliminate a variable;

nodes.title: Lesson 7-4
nodes.description: Solve systems of linear equations using elimination when multiplication of one or both equations is required;

nodes.title: Lesson 7-5
nodes.description: Graph systems of linear inequalities and identify the overlapping solution region (feasible region); ; Solve real-world optimization problems by defining variables, writing a system of inequalities, graphing, and interpreting the feasible region

nodes.title: Lesson 8-1
nodes.description: Identify exponential behavior from data using common ratios;

nodes.title: Lesson 8-2
nodes.description: Describe transformations of exponential functions verbally and in function notation; Write equations for transformed exponential functions given a graph

nodes.title: Lesson 8-3
nodes.description: Write an exponential function given two points on its graph; Apply the compound-interest formula to investment and depreciation problems

nodes.title: Lesson 8-4
nodes.description: Rewrite exponential functions so rates share the same time unit for direct comparison

nodes.title: Lesson 8-5
nodes.description: Determine whether a given sequence is geometric by checking for a constant common ratio; Write and use explicit formulas to model real-world situations involving exponential growth and decay

nodes.title: Lesson 8-6
nodes.description: Find the first several terms of a sequence given a recursive formula; Convert between recursive and explicit formulas for arithmetic and geometric sequences

nodes.description: Construct and complete two-way frequency tables from verbal descriptions or given data
